jobs.description: We are a fast-growing e-commerce company based in Sweden, operating globally with a strong focus on DTC and high-volume order fulfillment. We are looking for a skilled and proactive Shopify E-Commerce Manager to take ownership of our store and help us scale to the next level. This is not just a maintenance role — we want someone who can actively grow the business, improve conversion rates, and help launch new ideas and product pages. 🔧 Key Responsibilities: Manage and optimize our Shopify store (products, collections, apps, UX) Oversee inventory and product listings (we handle 100s of SKUs) Improve conversion rate (CRO) across product pages and cart Implement and execute SEO strategies (on-page, technical, content) Analyze performance data (Shopify, GA, ads) and identify growth opportunities Build and launch new landing pages, bundles, and offers Test new ideas (funnels, upsells, pricing strategies, etc.) Ensure smooth day-to-day operations of the store 🚀 What We’re Looking For: Strong experience with Shopify (themes, apps, backend) Proven track record in growing e-commerce stores Understanding of CRO, UX, and customer journey Data-driven mindset (comfortable with analytics & reporting) Ability to take initiative and execute ideas independently Experience with international stores is a plus 💡 Bonus Skills (Not Required but Valuable): Experience with subscriptions, bundles, or upsells Familiarity with Klaviyo or email marketing flows Basic design skills (Canva/Figma) Experience launching new product lines or stores 🌍 About Us: We are a rapidly scaling e-commerce brand with global customers and high order volume. We move fast, test constantly, and are always looking for new ways to grow. Important: When applying, please include: Examples of Shopify stores you have worked on What you would improve first on a store like ours A short explanation of your biggest e-commerce win
jobs.title: Shopify E-Commerce Manager (Growth-Focused, Fast Scaling Brand)

jobs.description: We are looking for an experienced bookkeeping professional to assist with Shopify and Amazon payout reconciliation within Xero. In this role, you will be responsible for reconciling payouts received from Shopify and Amazon and ensuring they are accurately recorded in Xero. This includes matching sales, fees, refunds, and deposits to bank transactions and ensuring all records are properly categorized and aligned. You will also be expected to identify and resolve discrepancies between platform reports and bank statements, ensuring that revenue, fees, and net payouts are correctly reflected in the financial records. The goal is to maintain accurate and clean bookkeeping for all ecommerce sales channels. We are looking for someone with strong experience in Xero and ecommerce bookkeeping, particularly with Shopify and Amazon reconciliation. Familiarity with payout structures, fees, and integrations is highly important. The ideal candidate should be detail-oriented, reliable, and able to work independently while maintaining accuracy in complex transaction environments.
jobs.title: Shopify/Amazon Payout Reconciliation

Will hope to find someone I can work with ongoing as the site will be added to weekly & will need ongoing advice. To Apply: Please answer: Your hourly rate Shopify stores you’ve worked on Your working hours A short example of how you explain things simply

jobs.description: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager (Growth & Revenue) - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand We’re looking for someone who knows how to turn traffic into profit. Not someone who just tweaks pages. Not someone who runs random A/B tests. We want a growth operator who understands: 👉 What makes people buy 👉 How to increase profit per visitor 👉 How to build offers, funnels, and systems that drive real revenue 🧠 About Us We’re a fast-growing DTC eCommerce brand doing ~$1M/month, primarily through Meta (Facebook) ads, targeting US/UK/CA/AU markets. Our product sits in a highly emotional niche (health / relief / transformation), where messaging, positioning, and belief systems drive conversions. You’ll be working directly with the founder and a creative strategist in a fast-moving, performance-driven environment. 🎯 Your Core Mission 👉 Increase profit per session, not just conversion rate This includes: Improving conversion rate Increasing AOV Expanding LTV Building stronger offers 🔥 What You’ll Be Responsible For 1. Offer & Monetization Optimization Build and test new offers, bundles, and pricing structures Increase AOV and upsell take rate Identify and implement cross-sells and post-purchase offers Suggest new products based on data and customer behavior 2. Landing Pages & Funnels Build and iterate on high-converting landing pages Translate winning ad angles into page structure, copy, and flow Create: New main landing page Angle-specific landing pages Advertorial funnels 3. CRO Testing Engine Run continuous A/B tests (minimum 2 live at all times) Use tools like Intelligems (and potentially Triple Whale) Identify: Bottlenecks Drop-off points Revenue leaks 4. Data → Insights → Revenue Turn data into clear, actionable insights Focus on: Profit per session Conversion rate AOV / upsell performance Work closely with: Creative strategist (messaging & angles) Founder (strategy & direction) 👉 You will connect ad performance with on-site monetization 5. Customer Journey Optimization Improve key pages: Product page Homepage About page Optimize the entire buying experience from click to purchase 6. AI-Driven Execution Use AI tools for: Copywriting Research Creative ideation Move fast from idea → test → result 🧩 Tools & Stack Shopify (custom Shrine theme) Replo Figma Intelligems Potentially Triple Whale 📈 90-Day Success Metrics Increase CVR from ~3% → 4%+ Increase profit per session Improve AOV and upsell take rate Launch: 1 new winning landing page 2 Iterations on top-performing landing page so they are angle-specific towards the top performing angles Maintain 2+ active tests at all times ✅ What We’re Looking For 3+ years in eCommerce (Shopify, DTC) Experience in English-speaking markets (US, UK, CA, AU) Strong understanding of: Direct response copywriting Landing page psychology Offer creation & pricing AOV / LTV optimization Ability to: Execute (not just strategize) Think in revenue, not just conversion rate Work fast and independently 🚫 This Is NOT For You If You only analyze data but don’t execute You rely on developers for every small change You’ve only worked on small or low-traffic stores You don’t understand offers, upsells, or monetization You don’t use AI to increase speed and output You don't know how to manage your time efficiently and focus only on the tasks that have the biggest ROI potential. 💰 Compensation $4,000–$5,000/month Part-time (20 hours/week) Performance-based bonus tied to: Profit per session Revenue growth Conversion improvements 🔥 Why This Role Is Different You’ll work on a high-volume store with real data You’ll have direct impact on revenue You’ll help shape offers, not just pages You’ll operate in a fast, no-BS environment focused on results 📩 How to Apply Include: 2–3 examples of stores or funnels you’ve worked on Specific results (CVR, AOV, revenue impact) An example of how you increased AOV or LTV Your approach to improving profit per session and LTV If you’ve read this far, include the word “PROFIT” in your application so we know you pay attention to detail.
jobs.title: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand

jobs.description: I’m looking for an experienced Shopify developer to turn a finished Figma design into a pixel-perfect, production-ready Shopify store. About the project: The brand is CELLARIS. The design work is already complete — both the full UI design and the brand book are ready to go in Figma. I just need someone to bring it to life on Shopify. Figma files (view access provided): • UI design: https://www.figma.com/design/JTaITL9meR46djZcJMgwN4/CELLARIS?node-id=207-575&t=C21IMM3RS4P8QH6G-1 • Brand book: https://www.figma.com/design/JTaITL9meR46djZcJMgwN4/CELLARIS?node-id=5-2&t=371EajDScOYuq2IA-1 Scope of work: • Build a custom Shopify theme (based on Dawn or from scratch — your recommendation) that matches the Figma design pixel-perfectly • Implement all key templates: Home, Collection, Product, Cart, Checkout styling, About, Contact, 404, and any other pages shown in Figma • Ensure the design is fully responsive (desktop, tablet, mobile) and matches the brand book (typography, colors, spacing, components) • Configure theme settings and sections so I can edit content in the Shopify customizer without touching code • Set up product templates, collections, and any custom product options shown in the design • Basic performance optimization (Lighthouse score 85+ on mobile) • Clean, well-commented Liquid code and documentation What you’ll need to deliver: • A fully working Shopify theme installed on my store • Any custom sections/blocks needed to match the design • Brief handover doc explaining how to edit key areas Requirements: • Strong portfolio of custom Shopify themes (please share 2–3 live stores you’ve built) • Fluent in Liquid, HTML, CSS/SCSS, and JavaScript • Experience translating Figma designs into Shopify 1:1 • Good communication and ability to estimate timeline upfront To apply: Please include: 1. 2–3 examples of Shopify stores you’ve built from 2. Start the message with the word “CELLARIS” so I know you read the full brief Looking forward to working with you.
jobs.title: Shopify Developer Needed for Figma to Shopify Conversion

Please include examples of similar projects you’ve completed and results.

jobs.description: About Us: We are Ideal Trading Limited, an established supplier of commercial kitchen, bakery, and refrigeration equipment based in Lami, Fiji. Since 2013, we have been supplying local restaurants, resorts, and businesses with high-quality commercial appliances, food preparation tools, and shelving units. We are currently expanding our digital presence and upgrading our backend operations (including Zoho Inventory integrations). We need an experienced SEO professional to optimize our website (idealtrading.com.fj) to capture high-intent, regional search traffic. Project Overview: Our website serves as a digital catalog for B2B buyers. The primary goal of this role is to improve our organic visibility for commercial equipment searches within Fiji and the broader South Pacific region including Vanuatu, Tonga, Solomon Islands etc. You will be responsible for conducting a complete technical audit, optimizing our category pages, and establishing a long-term local keyword strategy. Key Responsibilities: Comprehensive SEO Audit: Conduct an initial audit of idealtrading.com.fj to identify technical roadblocks, site speed issues, and indexing errors. Keyword Strategy & Mapping: Identify low-competition, high-intent B2B keywords specific to our market (e.g., "commercial refrigeration Fiji," "bakery equipment Suva," "industrial shelving units"). On-Page Optimization: Optimize title tags, meta descriptions, headers, and image alt-text across our main categories (Bakery Equipment, Kitchen Equipment, Shelving Units, Refrigeration, Food Service, etc.). Technical SEO: Ensure our e-commerce catalog structure is easily crawlable and properly indexed by Google. Local SEO: Optimize our local search presence to drive warehouse pickups and direct inquiries via WhatsApp/Viber/Email. Monthly Reporting: Track rankings, organic traffic growth, and conversion metrics using Google Search Console and Google Analytics.
jobs.title: SEO for Ideal Trading

visually (colors, fonts, layout, flow) Mobile-first and fully responsive design Experience designing for lifestyle or niche brands (bonus) 🛒 Store Setup & Optimization Shopify theme customization (or recommendation + build) Homepage, product page, collection page, and landing page design Navigation structure that makes shopping easy and intuitive Product page optimization (images, layout, trust elements, etc.) App integrations (reviews, upsells, email capture, etc.) Basic SEO structure (meta titles, descriptions, site structure) 🚀 Phase 2 (Ongoing Opportunity): MARKETING & GROWTH If Phase 1 goes well, I would love to continue working together long-term to grow the brand and drive sales. 📈 Marketing & Sales Growth Conversion rate optimization (CRO) Email marketing setup (Klaviyo or similar) Funnel creation (landing pages, lead magnets, upsells) Product launch strategy 🔍 SEO & Traffic On-page SEO optimization Keyword research for products and collections Blog/content strategy (if applicable) 📣 Paid & Organic Marketing (Bonus) Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ads TikTok Shop or TikTok Ads experience Influencer or affiliate marketing strategy 🎯 Ideal Candidate Strong portfolio of Shopify stores (please include links) Design-forward and understands eCommerce strategy Experience building stores that actually generate sales Communicates clearly and proactively Thinks like a partner, not just a freelancer 💡 Project Goal To build a beautiful, trustworthy, and high-converting Shopify store, then scale it into a revenue-generating brand through strategic marketing and Let’s build something great.

jobs.description: We are a U.S.-based eCommerce business currently operating on Shopify at an 8-figure annual revenue scale, seeking a highly experienced Payment Processing Specialist to help onboard and stabilize a high-risk merchant setup. We are open to modifying or restructuring our current setup as needed to ensure successful onboarding and long-term processing stability. Our goal is to establish a reliable, scalable payments infrastructure with the right long-term partner. What You’ll Do: Identify and secure appropriate high-risk merchant accounts (U.S. domestic preferred) Lead underwriting strategy, including application positioning and documentation Advise on and implement structural changes (business model, checkout flow, entity setup, etc.) to improve approval odds Design and deploy a multi-processor environment (primary + backup redundancy) Integrate and optimize gateways compatible with Shopify or alternative setups if needed Develop routing strategies to maximize uptime and approval rates Implement chargeback mitigation, fraud prevention, and risk controls Ensure compliance and long-term sustainability across all processing channels Requirements: Proven success onboarding high-risk, high-volume merchants Strong relationships with acquiring banks, ISOs, and payment processors Deep understanding of underwriting, MCC classification, and risk thresholds Experience with Shopify and/or alternative eCommerce/payment architectures Expertise in multi-processor setups, load balancing, and failover systems Knowledge of compliance, disputes, and fraud/risk management tools Ability to operate discreetly and professionally What We’re Looking For: A true expert with hands-on experience—not a general consultant Someone focused on securing stable, long-term processing (not short-term approvals) Strategic thinker who can adapt and restructure systems for approval success A reliable partner for ongoing optimization and growth Engagement Details: Remote, flexible engagement (project-based or long-term partnership) Competitive compensation based on experience and results Open to restructuring our setup to achieve a stable processing solution How to Apply: Please share your experience onboarding high-risk merchants, including examples of restructuring or repositioning businesses for approval. Include processors/banks you’ve worked with and measurable results (e.g., approvals secured)
jobs.title: High Risk Merchant Payment Processing Onboarding

jobs.description: US-based bookkeeping firm specializing in Shopify e-commerce and short-term rental clients. We use QuickBooks Online, A2X, and OwnerREZ. This starts as a small test project. If the work is clean and accurate, it moves into an ongoing part-time engagement at 10 hrs/week and growing. We need help with: A2X integrations — setting up and troubleshooting Shopify-to-QBO mapping OwnerREZ integrations — QBO sync, channel splits (Airbnb/VRBO/direct), cleaning fees, pet fees, security deposits, platform fees New QBO file setups from scratch Financial cleanups on existing books Complex categorization and multi-entity work Not a data entry role. Basic reconciliation is already covered. We need someone technical who can handle complex work independently, not just from a checklist. You're a fit if: 3+ years QBO experience (certification preferred) You've actually set up A2X integrations before You've worked with OwnerREZ, Airbnb, or VRBO reconciliation You communicate clearly in English and flag problems early You can work from documented SOPs without hand-holding You're available for occasional check-ins during US Pacific hours Compensation: Paid through Upwork. Contractor engagement. Quote your hourly rate in your application. How we evaluate: Applications are reviewed on the strength of your video, written answers, and rate. Top applicants are invited to a paid test project at your quoted rate, capped at 2 hours, which leads into the ongoing engagement if the work is strong. We review applications within 10 business days and respond to every candidate who meets the minimum requirements. Written questions — please submit answers with your application: Question A (Shopify/A2X): A Shopify client's bank deposit shows $4,200 from Shopify. Their dashboard shows $6,100 in gross sales. Why are those numbers different, and how do you record the payout correctly in QBO? Question B (OwnerREZ/STR): A short-term rental host has three properties and takes bookings through Airbnb, VRBO, and direct. Their CPA wants separate QBO company files per property. Walk through how you'd set up one of those files and correctly map cleaning fees, pet fees, security deposits, and channel fees. To apply, send: A 2–3 minute Loom video introducing yourself and your relevant experience. In your video, please share one example of a time you caught a bookkeeping issue before it became a problem. Written answers to Question A and Question B Your hourly rate Applications without a video and written answers will not be reviewed.
jobs.title: QBO Specialist — Shopify (A2X) + Short-Term Rental (OwnerREZ)

jobs.description: We’re looking for an experienced Shopify, Klaviyo, and SEO specialist to support the structured shutdown and transition of an ecommerce brand. jobs.title: Shopify Migration / E-commerce Shutdown Specialist

jobs.description: We're in the final stretch of a full Shopify store rebuild and need a technical operator to handle the backend build-out before launch. This is a focused 30-day build. If the work is clean and the communication is tight, there's a strong possibility of ongoing retainer work post-launch (flow optimization, new campaigns, bundle iterations, TikTok Shop maintenance). We are not looking for a strategist, copywriter, or designer. Our team handles strategy, brand, and copy. We need someone who builds fast, builds correctly, and knows the platforms cold. What You'll Build Klaviyo (full flow architecture from scratch) ✅ Welcome series ✅ Abandoned cart ✅ Browse abandonment ✅ Post-purchase ✅ Win-back ✅ Replenishment ✅ Segmentation setup and list hygiene ✅ Integration with Shopify (events, properties, product feed) Shopify ✅ Automation and workflow setup (order routing, tagging, inventory sync) ✅ Any technical cleanup needed to support the flows above TikTok Shop ✅ Account is already approved. Products are not. ✅ Submit catalog for product approval ✅ Sync Shopify catalog to TikTok Shop ✅ Configure order and fulfillment routing back to Shopify Who We're Looking For 🔹 Proven Klaviyo experience building full flow architecture for e-commerce brands (supplement or health/wellness experience is a strong plus) 🔹 Direct Shopify backend fluency (bundles, automations, app integrations, catalog management) 🔹 TikTok Shop experience specifically (you've submitted products for approval before and know how to navigate the process) 🔹 Ability to work independently, communicate proactively, and hit deadlines 🔹 Based anywhere, but reliable overlap with U.S. Central Time for async check-ins Timeline 📅 30-day build starting as soon as we onboard the right person How to Apply Do not send a written proposal. We will not read them. Send a Loom video (5 minutes or less) covering: 1️⃣ Stores you've worked on in the backend (walk us through them on screen) 2️⃣ Klaviyo flows you've built (show us the account if possible) 3️⃣ One sentence on your TikTok Shop experience Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed. We're moving fast and need to hire within the next few days.
jobs.title: Shopify + Klaviyo Technical Operator Needed for Supplement Brand (30-Day Build)

jobs.description: I am looking for a Shopify-focused Operations Expert to finalize a "Single Hub" workflow. My current setup is a mix of manual and disconnected automated processes; I need a professional to consolidate these into a streamlined system centered on Shopify. Key Tasks: • POD Re-Routing: Currently, sales on my established Etsy store are disconnected from my POD providers (Printify/Gelato). You must set up the path so Etsy (ebay etc) orders flow through the new Shopify to the correct POD provider automatically • Order Flow Validation: Verify that sales from my established Etsy/eBay/Amazon stores successfully flow into Shopify and trigger the correct Print-on-Demand (Printify/Gelato) fulfillment path. Where we don’t yet have products, let’s set up a template product. • SKU Mapping & Integration: Products were brought to Shopify from Etys using Lit Comm. Just need to confirm that’s complete and that sku’s map to defined structure • Collection Automation: Review and fix Shopify "Automated Collection" logic to ensure products are categorized correctly based on tags/types. • Low-Subscription Workflow: I want to avoid ongoing sync software like LitCommerce. I prefer a manual or "on-demand" batch process for seasonal catalog updates. You will recommend and implement a cost-effective method for this. • Hub Management: Finalize connections for eBay, Amazon, and Etsy. Ensure the shop is ready to scale to IG and Walmart in the future. • Shopify Store Audit: Verify the data migration and confirm that all necessary backend pages (policies, etc.) are functional. (No web design required). • Subscription Cleanup: Identify and remove unnecessary middleman apps; recommend low-cost/free alternatives for seasonal batch updates. • SOP: Provide process hand off notes for maintenance (short form) Requirements: • Expertise in Shopify Marketplace Connect or manual SKU linking. • Deep knowledge of Shopify Automated Collections (tag-based logic). • Experience with multi-vendor POD routing (Printify + Gelato). • Upwork Only. No off-site interactions. Availability Note: I am hiring because I lack the time to do this myself. I will not be immediately available for chat but will schedule regular check-ins and review cycles to answer questions.
jobs.title: Shopify Operations Expert: Integration & Marketplace Linking (Etsy/eBay/Amazon)

jobs.description: We are seeking an experienced data engineer or automation specialist to build a fully automated, low-maintenance CEO dashboard. The goal is to provide a single "pane of glass" overview of our business performance (Trade, Ecommerce, Marketplaces, and Whole Company metrics) on a daily and weekly basis. The data currently lives across nine distinct platforms: Xero, Cin7, Amazon Sellerboard, A2X, Triple Whale, Shopify, HubSpot, Airwallex, and Cashflow Frog. Crucial Requirement: We want to avoid overcomplicating the architecture. The solution must be low-bug, easy to work with, highly reliable, and manageable in-house after handover. We already use Make.com and strongly prefer leveraging it as the orchestration layer to pull data into a central repository (like Google Sheets) and visualize it (e.g., Looker Studio or advanced Google Sheets charts). Note: While Make.com is our preferred approach for simplicity and maintainability, we are open to alternative architectures (e.g., Fivetran + BigQuery, or other off-the-shelf connectors) if you can make a compelling case that it will be more stable, cost-effective, and easy for a non-developer team to manage long-term. The freelancer will be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of the dashboard, specifically: 1 Architecture Design: Finalize the data flow. Our proposed baseline is using Make.com to query the APIs on a schedule, transform the JSON responses, and append the daily/weekly KPI values into a structured Google Sheet, which then feeds Looker Studio. 2 Data Extraction & Integration: Set up the Make.com scenarios (or alternative pipelines) for the 7 APIs. Handle OAuth authentication flows (especially Xero and HubSpot) and secure credential storage. 3 Data Transformation: Ensure metrics like "Total Orders" accurately combine Shopify, Amazon, and Cin7 data without double-counting. 4 Dashboard Build: Design and build the visualization layer. It must be clean, executive-level, and easy to read on desktop and tablet. 5 Cashflow Frog Workaround: Implement a solution for the 30-Day Cash Outlook, either by having Make.com parse scheduled Excel exports from Cashflow Frog or by recreating the calculation logic within the dashboard. 6 Handover & Documentation: Provide a recorded walkthrough and written documentation on how to maintain the Make.com scenarios, update API keys if they expire, and make minor changes to charts. Ideal Candidate Profile We are looking for a freelancer with the following qualifications: • Proven experience building executive dashboards using Looker Studio, Power BI, or advanced Google Sheets. • Strong experience integrating with accounting and ecommerce APIs, specifically Xero, Shopify, and HubSpot. • Deep expertise in Make.com (Integromat) for complex API orchestration and data transformation. • A pragmatic approach to problem-solving—favoring robust, maintainable solutions over complex, over-engineered systems. • Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
jobs.title: Build a CEO dashboard including multi-platform integration

All the backend products have been added on shopify. I have all the visual designs on squarespace. Now we just need to transition them over.

jobs.description: I need a one-page website built for GODFUCKINGSPEED.COM. The book is already live on Amazon. I need this designed, built, and ready to launch within 48 hours. **PLATFORM** Build this on Shopify unless you have a faster, cleaner option that still supports direct digital sales, email capture, and future product expansion. The site must support: - Direct digital sales - Email capture - Easy product additions later **DESIGN DIRECTION** Black background. Gold and brass text. Dark red accent buttons. Serif font for headlines. Clean sans-serif for body text. The visual energy should match the attached book cover and interior part images as closely as possible. This is a sovereign publishing imprint. Not a blog. Not a portfolio. It should feel like opening a classified file. **STRUCTURE** One page. Six sections. Vertical scroll. No sidebar. No clutter. **SECTION 1 — HERO** Full-screen book cover image on a dark background. Text below the image: THE PLANT OF REMEMBRANCE Volume I of The Sovereign Archives The record was sealed in 1937. This book opens it. Two buttons side by side: BUY ON AMAZON — $9.99 Links to Amazon listing BUY DIRECT — $11.99 Includes the Archive Briefing PDF Digital download through site checkout **SECTION 2 — DESCRIPTION** They did not just ban a plant. They erased the memory that it was ever given. Five thousand years of documented medicine. Exposed in one file. The government classified it as having no medical use. The government holds a patent claiming medical use for its compounds. Both are true. At the same time. Right now. The ancient texts confirmed it. The receptor system proved the body was built for it. The archaeological residue on a twenty-seven-hundred-year-old altar showed it was sacred. The patent showed they knew. The enforcement data showed who paid the price. The pharmaceutical economics showed who profited. The pieces were scattered across disciplines that never talk to each other. This book is the assembly. 33 chapters. Three parts. One plant. One file. The record was buried. The theft was surgical. The return is inevitable. **SECTION 3 — THE EVIDENCE** This section should feel like a stripped-down evidence board. Short lines. Hard facts. No paragraph formatting. 1937. Less than two pages of congressional debate. A plant used as medicine for five millennia made criminal overnight. Patent 6630507. Filed by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services. Claims medical use of cannabinoid compounds. Held by the same government that classifies the plant as having none. The endocannabinoid system. Discovered in 1992. Named after the plant. Built into every human body on earth. Not taught in most medical schools. Tel Arad. Eighth century BC. Cannabis residue identified on a functioning altar by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry. Published 2020. Peer reviewed. Eight million arrests in one decade. The ACLU documented the enforcement. The pattern was not random. The record exists. This book assembles it. **SECTION 4 — EMAIL CAPTURE** This file is not closed. One email input field. One button labeled ENTER THE ARCHIVE. Connect this to Klaviyo or Mailchimp. I will provide account details. **SECTION 5 — SERIES PREVIEW** Display three part images side by side: THE RECORD | THE THEFT | THE RETURN Below the images: THE SOVEREIGN ARCHIVES Seven sealed files. One vault. Volume I — THE PLANT OF REMEMBRANCE — The file is open. Volume II — THE SPIRAL OF FORGETTING — Sealed. Volume III — THE SIGHT OF BETRAYAL — Sealed. Volume IV — THE RESTORATION OF FOUNDATION — Sealed. Volume V — THE RISE OF SOVEREIGNTY — Sealed. Volume VI — THE BLOODLINE LEGACY — Sealed. Volume VII — THE CROWN OF DOMINION — Sealed. **SECTION 6 — FOOTER** GODFUCKINGSPEED PUBLISHING An Imprint of DOMUS VILLARREAL © 2026 Daniel GREENBEAST Villarreal. All Rights Reserved. **ASSETS I WILL PROVIDE** - High-resolution book cover image - Three part images - Amazon listing URL - Digital ebook file for direct checkout - Email service account details **DELIVERABLES** - Fully built one-page site - Mobile responsive - Fast loading - SSL secure - Direct checkout working - Email capture connected - All copy used exactly as written **BUDGET** $100 for a built draft or send your best price for full live deployment to GODFUCKINGSPEED.COM **DEADLINE** 48 hours
jobs.title: One-Page Publishing Website Build - Direct Sales & Email Capture - 24 Hours

jobs.description: I am looking for an experienced Shopify developer to build a small, lightweight custom app or script to solve a limitation with the Shopify Facebook/Instagram sales channel. Currently, the discount codes automatically generated by the FB/IG channel do not allow customers to combine them with shipping discounts. I need a custom Shopify solution developed to automate this adjustment so my customers can successfully check out using both discounts. **How the App Should Work:** The solution needs to automatically "watch" my Shopify store for new discount codes generated by the FB/IG sales channel. * Whenever a new code is created (typically formatted like `FB-Email - [code]`), the app must instantly update its settings via the Shopify Admin API to **allow combining with shipping discounts**. * This needs to happen automatically in the background with zero manual work required on my end. **Key Requirements & Expected Behavior:** 🔸 **Maintain Exclusivity:** Each customer still gets their own unique, one-time-use code (as originally intended by the sales channel) to keep fraud/sharing risks low. 🔸 **Fully Automated:** Once set up, the process must be 100% hands-off. 🔸 **Tech Stack:** You can build this as a custom/private Shopify app or a serverless script utilizing Shopify Webhooks and the Admin API (GraphQL , and lightweight solution.
jobs.title: Custom Shopify App Development for Discount Updates
